blockingIssues.description: The direct `zod` dependency fix improves `apps/mcp-server` launchability when hoisting is absent, but it does not resolve the prior `mcp-codex-integration` scrutiny blocker. The project still lacks Codex-owned discovery/tool-visibility evidence and only validates integration by parsing `.codex/config.toml` locally and spawning the server directly.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update REQUIRED MCP manual verification to match `.factory/library/user-testing.md`: use the real handshake (`initialize` -> `notifications/initialized` -> `tools/list`) and require at least one feature-relevant `tools/call`; for error-handling/protocol work, explicitly probe malformed JSON-RPC envelopes such as `[]`, `{}`, and `{"foo":"bar"}`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Rerun reviews for `fix-mcp-head-verification`, `fix-mcp-error-context`, and `fix-mcp-jsonrpc-envelope` all observed workers following the current initialize/tools-list-only skill guidance, which no longer matches the stronger MCP validation procedure already documented in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to set `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` and record deviations whenever they skip the documented TDD/build/manual-verification steps, instead of reporting the procedure as followed after only automated validators.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-mcp-head-verification` rerun still found a handoff marked `followedProcedure=true` even though only test/typecheck/lint were run and no manual stdio verification was recorded, so the current skill-following signal remains unreliable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a lighter-weight dependency/config-fix procedure so manifest-only or packaging-only MCP fixes can use proportional targeted validation rather than the full new-tool workflow, while still requiring relevant verification for launchability changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-mcp-zod-dep` review found the worker was pushed through the full new-tool workflow even though the change was only a direct dependency declaration; the handoff explicitly requested a lighter dependency/config-fix path in `mcp-worker` guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add another library note about the MCP handshake and feature-relevant tool-call verification.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`; the remaining gap is worker-skill guidance, not missing factual library knowledge

rejectedObservations.observation: Add another architecture/library note about runtime-bridged MCP handlers or repo-root launch assumptions.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md`, so no additional shared-state update is needed
